It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue ware, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue ware, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == * It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}. The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue ware, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue ware, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue ware, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == * Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue ware, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue ware, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue ware, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue ware, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue ware, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == * {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == * Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == * Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == * This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == * {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == * Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |artifacts = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == * It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |burial goods = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == * This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alias = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |lat_deg = 34 |lat_min = 35 |lat_sec = 36.03 |long_deg = 135 |long_min = 50 |long_sec = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |burial goods = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Circular mound |label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the shape of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. Artifacts discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later artifacts, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == *
