{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alternate_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 36.03 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |burial goods = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |type = Circular mound |map_dot_label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} and takes the type of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukaanayama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Tsukaana-yama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tennōji history]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan tombs]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] [[sn:sukaanayama Kofun]]

The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukaanayama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Tsukaana-yama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tennōji history]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan tombs]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] [[sn:sukaanayama Kofun]]

Overview of the stone chamber |alternate_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 36.03 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |burial goods = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |type = Circular mound |map_dot_label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} and takes the type of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukaanayama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Tsukaana-yama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tennōji history]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan tombs]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] [[sn:sukaanayama Kofun]]

The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukaanayama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Tsukaana-yama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tennōji history]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan tombs]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] [[sn:sukaanayama Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses||奈良県香芝市にある古墳|平野塚穴山古墳}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 塚穴山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
石室全景 |別名 = |所属 = [[杣之内古墳群]] |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]勾田町字塚穴山 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 36.03 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 31.60 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径63.4m（推定復元）
高さ8m以上 |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（石舞台式）
（内部に石棺） |出土品 = 鉄刀・鉄鎌・[[須恵器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]代 |被葬者 = （一説）物部大市御狩 |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 塚穴山古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|35|36|N|135|50|45|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height =200 | caption = {{center|塚穴山古墳・峯塚古墳・杣之内火葬墓
分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|35|36.03|N|135|50|31.60|E}} | label1=塚穴山古墳 | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=Red pog.svg | mark-size1=10 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|38.25|N|135|50|57.27|E}} | label2=峯塚古墳 | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=Red pog.svg | mark-size2=10 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|35|17.40|N|135|51|4.73|E}} | label3=杣之内火葬墓 | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=Red pog.svg | mark-size3=10 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|35|52|N|135|51|7|E}} | label4=石上神宮 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=Blue pog.svg | mark-size4=10 }} '''塚穴山古墳'''（つかあなやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]勾田町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[杣之内古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]東縁、[[西山古墳]]北側の丘陵（堤）端を切り込んで築造された大型円墳である{{Sfn|塚穴山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。これまでに墳丘北半および上半部が破壊されて石室が露出しているほか、[[1964年]]（[[昭和]]39年）・[[1988年]]（昭和63年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、推定復元で直径60メートル以上（推定復元63.4メートル）を測り、当該時期の円墳としては全国でも有数の規模になる{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。墳丘周囲には幅9.5-13メートルの周濠および外堤が巡らされており、周濠・外堤を含めた外径は112メートルにもおよぶ{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、巨石を用いた大型石室であり、南方向に開口する{{Sfn|塚穴山古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。[[石舞台古墳]]（[[明日香村]]）に類似する石舞台式石室になり、石室全長は17.12メートルを測り全国でも有数の規模になる{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。盗掘に遭っているため副葬品のほとんどは失われているが、発掘調査では鉄刀片などが検出されている{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]代と推定される{{Sfn|塚穴山古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。[[終末期古墳]]の中でも墳丘・石室が最大級の規模になる点で注目される古墳である。付近では大型の終末期古墳として[[峯塚古墳]]（天理市杣之内町、塚穴山古墳の後続古墳）も知られるほか、[[奈良時代]]の墓として精巧な[[海獣葡萄鏡]]が副葬された[[杣之内火葬墓]]があり、これらを[[物部氏]]の首長墓とする説が挙げられている[[白石太一郎]] 「古墳からみた物部氏」『古墳の被葬者を推理する（中公叢書）』 中央公論新社、2018年、pp. 253-260。日野宏『物部氏の拠点集落 布留遺跡（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」140）』新泉社、2019年、pp. 80-88。。特に本古墳については物部大市御狩（[[物部守屋]]の兄）の墓に比定する説などがある。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[中世]]頃、墓地として二次利用{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * [[1893年]]（[[明治]]26年）の『大和國古墳墓取調書』に絵図。[[前方後円墳]]と認識{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * [[1964年|1964]]-[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]39-40年）、測量・発掘調査：第1次調査。[[円墳]]と確認（[[天理大学附属天理参考館]]、[[1969年]]に報告）。 * [[1988年]]（昭和63年）、航空測量・発掘調査：第2次調査（埋蔵文化財天理教調査団、[[1989年|1989]]・[[1990年]]に一部報告、[[2023年]]に報告）。 * [[2013年]]度（[[平成]]25年度）、墳丘測量・石室実測調査（[[天理大学]]・[[帝塚山大学]]、[[2015年]]に報告）{{Sfn|天理市塚穴山古墳2014年測量調査概要|2015}}。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|周濠}}{{small|右に墳丘、左に外堤。}}]] 墳丘は円形で、現在までに大きく破壊を受けているが、元々は直径60メートル以上・高さ8メートル以上を測ったとされる{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}（かつて明治以降に前方後円墳とされた時期もあった{{Sfn|藤原郁代|2022}}）。1964年（昭和39年）調査では直径63.4メートルと推定復元されているほか、墳丘周囲の周濠は幅9.5メートル、外堤は幅14.9メートルを測ると報告されており、周濠・外堤を含めた外径は約112メートルにおよぶ{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 当該時期の大型円墳としては[[藤ノ木古墳]]（[[斑鳩町]]、直径48メートル）・[[牧野古墳]]（[[広陵町]]、直径45メートル）・[[ムネサカ1号墳]]（桜井市、直径45メートル）・[[叡福寺北古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]、直径54メートル）が知られるほか、大型方墳としては[[石舞台古墳]]（[[明日香村]]、一辺約50メートル）・[[ハミ塚古墳]]（天理市、一辺約50メートル）が知られ、塚穴山古墳は特に円墳としては群を抜く規模になる{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * 石室全長：17.12メートル * 玄室：長さ7.04メートル、幅2.9-3.24メートル、現在高さ3.6メートル * 羨道：長さ約9.4メートル（東壁）・約10メートル（西壁）、幅2.14-2.24メートル、現在高さ約3メートル 石室は[[花崗岩]]の巨石が使用された大型石室になるが、現在までに玄室・羨道の天井石と側壁上部が失われている。玄室の奥壁は現在では2段積みで上石は内傾し、側壁の基底石は東壁・西壁とも2石である{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。玄室の床面は石敷で、特に玄門付近には大石を敷き、床面周囲には幅20センチメートル・深さ30センチメートルの溝が巡らされる{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。羨道の床面は礫敷である{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。また床面下には排水用暗渠が構築されており、玄室奥から羨道中央を縦貫して周濠付近まで達する{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 石室の形態としては石舞台古墳と同様の特徴を有しており、「石舞台式石室」と捉えられる。当該時期の大型石室としては石舞台古墳（全長19メートル）・[[岩屋山古墳]]（明日香村、全長18メートル）・[[茅原狐塚古墳]]（桜井市、全長17メートル）・ムネサカ1号墳（全長17メートル）・牧野古墳（全長16メートル）・藤ノ木古墳（全長15メートル）が知られ、塚穴山古墳は有数の規模になる{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|俯瞰図 File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（羨道方向） File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 == 出土品 == 1964年（昭和39年）調査では、石室内からは凝灰岩製石棺片（内側に朱を塗布）・土器類（[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]）・銀糸捲鉄刀把片（所在不明）・鉄鎌が、墳丘からは[[埴輪]]片（[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪・家形埴輪）が出土している{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。また後世の遺物として、石室内から焼骨・中世土器類・石造物（五輪塔・石仏・板碑）が出土しているほか、墳丘からは中世墓6基も検出されており、中世期に墓地として利用されたことが知られる{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 1988年（昭和63年）調査では、周濠などから須恵器（TK47-TK209型式期）が出土している{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 現在では出土品の大半は[[天理大学附属天理参考館]]（天理市守目堂町）で保管されている{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 ファイル:塚穴山古墳出土 石棺片.JPG|石棺片
{{small|天理ギャラリー企画展示時に撮影。}} ファイル:塚穴山古墳出土 土師器 高坏 (紀伊風土記の丘展示).JPG|土師器 高坏
{{small|[[和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘]]企画展示時に撮影。}} ファイル:塚穴山古墳出土 二次遺物.JPG|後世の遺物群
{{small|天理ギャラリー企画展示時に撮影。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=塚穴山古墳|title=天理市の文化財|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|塚穴山古墳（天理市の文化財）|1990}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=塚穴山古墳|title=天理の古墳100|publisher=天理市教育委員会|pages=|ref={{Harvid|塚穴山古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=塚穴山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|塚穴山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=藤原郁代 |title=塚穴山古墳について |publisher=杣之内古墳群研究会 |year=2014-03 |NCID=BB15553252 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/74013 |id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|025447652}}}}, {{全国書誌番号|22415717}} ** {{Cite journal|和書|author=桑原久男, 小田木治太郎, 岸田徹, 寺村裕史, 宇野隆夫 |title=天理市塚穴山古墳2014年測量調査概要 |journal=古事 : 天理大学考古学・民俗学研究室紀要 |ISSN=1346-8847 |publisher=天理大学考古学研究室 |date=2015-03|volume=19 |pages=76-92 |naid=120005858504 |url=https://opac.tenri-u.ac.jp/opac/repository/metadata/3463/ |ref={{Harvid|天理市塚穴山古墳2014年測量調査概要|2015}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=藤原郁代|year=2022|chapter=[https://opac.tenri-u.ac.jp/opac/repository/metadata/4849/ 塚穴山古墳出土資料の検討]|title= 杣之内古墳群の研究II|publisher=天理大学|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|藤原郁代|2022}}}} ** {{Cite book |和書|chapter= 塚穴山古墳第2次調査 |title=塚穴山古墳第2次・杣之内古墳群須川地区発掘調査報告書（考古学調査研究報告33）|year=2023 |publisher=埋蔵文化財天理教調査団 |doi=}} == 関連文献 == revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[塚穴山古墳]] to [[Tsukaanayama Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{Otheruses||奈良県香芝市にある古墳|平野塚穴山古墳}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 塚穴山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
石室全景 |別名 = |所属 = [[杣之内古墳群]] |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]勾田町字塚穴山 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 36.03 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 31.60 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径63.4m（推定復元）
高さ8m以上 |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（石舞台式）
（内部に石棺） |出土品 = 鉄刀・鉄鎌・[[須恵器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]代 |被葬者 = （一説）物部大市御狩 |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 塚穴山古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|35|36|N|135|50|45|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height =200 | caption = {{center|塚穴山古墳・峯塚古墳・杣之内火葬墓
分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|35|36.03|N|135|50|31.60|E}} | label1=塚穴山古墳 | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=Red pog.svg | mark-size1=10 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|38.25|N|135|50|57.27|E}} | label2=峯塚古墳 | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=Red pog.svg | mark-size2=10 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|35|17.40|N|135|51|4.73|E}} | label3=杣之内火葬墓 | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=Red pog.svg | mark-size3=10 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|35|52|N|135|51|7|E}} | label4=石上神宮 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=Blue pog.svg | mark-size4=10 }} '''塚穴山古墳'''（つかあなやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]勾田町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[杣之内古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]東縁、[[西山古墳]]北側の丘陵（堤）端を切り込んで築造された大型円墳である{{Sfn|塚穴山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。これまでに墳丘北半および上半部が破壊されて石室が露出しているほか、[[1964年]]（[[昭和]]39年）・[[1988年]]（昭和63年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、推定復元で直径60メートル以上（推定復元63.4メートル）を測り、当該時期の円墳としては全国でも有数の規模になる{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。墳丘周囲には幅9.5-13メートルの周濠および外堤が巡らされており、周濠・外堤を含めた外径は112メートルにもおよぶ{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、巨石を用いた大型石室であり、南方向に開口する{{Sfn|塚穴山古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。[[石舞台古墳]]（[[明日香村]]）に類似する石舞台式石室になり、石室全長は17.12メートルを測り全国でも有数の規模になる{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。盗掘に遭っているため副葬品のほとんどは失われているが、発掘調査では鉄刀片などが検出されている{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]代と推定される{{Sfn|塚穴山古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。[[終末期古墳]]の中でも墳丘・石室が最大級の規模になる点で注目される古墳である。付近では大型の終末期古墳として[[峯塚古墳]]（天理市杣之内町、塚穴山古墳の後続古墳）も知られるほか、[[奈良時代]]の墓として精巧な[[海獣葡萄鏡]]が副葬された[[杣之内火葬墓]]があり、これらを[[物部氏]]の首長墓とする説が挙げられている[[白石太一郎]] 「古墳からみた物部氏」『古墳の被葬者を推理する（中公叢書）』 中央公論新社、2018年、pp. 253-260。日野宏『物部氏の拠点集落 布留遺跡（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」140）』新泉社、2019年、pp. 80-88。。特に本古墳については物部大市御狩（[[物部守屋]]の兄）の墓に比定する説などがある。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[中世]]頃、墓地として二次利用{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * [[1893年]]（[[明治]]26年）の『大和國古墳墓取調書』に絵図。[[前方後円墳]]と認識{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * [[1964年|1964]]-[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]39-40年）、測量・発掘調査：第1次調査。[[円墳]]と確認（[[天理大学附属天理参考館]]、[[1969年]]に報告）。 * [[1988年]]（昭和63年）、航空測量・発掘調査：第2次調査（埋蔵文化財天理教調査団、[[1989年|1989]]・[[1990年]]に一部報告、[[2023年]]に報告）。 * [[2013年]]度（[[平成]]25年度）、墳丘測量・石室実測調査（[[天理大学]]・[[帝塚山大学]]、[[2015年]]に報告）{{Sfn|天理市塚穴山古墳2014年測量調査概要|2015}}。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|周濠}}{{small|右に墳丘、左に外堤。}}]] 墳丘は円形で、現在までに大きく破壊を受けているが、元々は直径60メートル以上・高さ8メートル以上を測ったとされる{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}（かつて明治以降に前方後円墳とされた時期もあった{{Sfn|藤原郁代|2022}}）。1964年（昭和39年）調査では直径63.4メートルと推定復元されているほか、墳丘周囲の周濠は幅9.5メートル、外堤は幅14.9メートルを測ると報告されており、周濠・外堤を含めた外径は約112メートルにおよぶ{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 当該時期の大型円墳としては[[藤ノ木古墳]]（[[斑鳩町]]、直径48メートル）・[[牧野古墳]]（[[広陵町]]、直径45メートル）・[[ムネサカ1号墳]]（桜井市、直径45メートル）・[[叡福寺北古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]、直径54メートル）が知られるほか、大型方墳としては[[石舞台古墳]]（[[明日香村]]、一辺約50メートル）・[[ハミ塚古墳]]（天理市、一辺約50メートル）が知られ、塚穴山古墳は特に円墳としては群を抜く規模になる{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * 石室全長：17.12メートル * 玄室：長さ7.04メートル、幅2.9-3.24メートル、現在高さ3.6メートル * 羨道：長さ約9.4メートル（東壁）・約10メートル（西壁）、幅2.14-2.24メートル、現在高さ約3メートル 石室は[[花崗岩]]の巨石が使用された大型石室になるが、現在までに玄室・羨道の天井石と側壁上部が失われている。玄室の奥壁は現在では2段積みで上石は内傾し、側壁の基底石は東壁・西壁とも2石である{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。玄室の床面は石敷で、特に玄門付近には大石を敷き、床面周囲には幅20センチメートル・深さ30センチメートルの溝が巡らされる{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。羨道の床面は礫敷である{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。また床面下には排水用暗渠が構築されており、玄室奥から羨道中央を縦貫して周濠付近まで達する{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 石室の形態としては石舞台古墳と同様の特徴を有しており、「石舞台式石室」と捉えられる。当該時期の大型石室としては石舞台古墳（全長19メートル）・[[岩屋山古墳]]（明日香村、全長18メートル）・[[茅原狐塚古墳]]（桜井市、全長17メートル）・ムネサカ1号墳（全長17メートル）・牧野古墳（全長16メートル）・藤ノ木古墳（全長15メートル）が知られ、塚穴山古墳は有数の規模になる{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|俯瞰図 File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（羨道方向） File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 == 出土品 == 1964年（昭和39年）調査では、石室内からは凝灰岩製石棺片（内側に朱を塗布）・土器類（[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]）・銀糸捲鉄刀把片（所在不明）・鉄鎌が、墳丘からは[[埴輪]]片（[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪・家形埴輪）が出土している{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。また後世の遺物として、石室内から焼骨・中世土器類・石造物（五輪塔・石仏・板碑）が出土しているほか、墳丘からは中世墓6基も検出されており、中世期に墓地として利用されたことが知られる{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 1988年（昭和63年）調査では、周濠などから須恵器（TK47-TK209型式期）が出土している{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 現在では出土品の大半は[[天理大学附属天理参考館]]（天理市守目堂町）で保管されている{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 ファイル:塚穴山古墳出土 石棺片.JPG|石棺片
{{small|天理ギャラリー企画展示時に撮影。}} ファイル:塚穴山古墳出土 土師器 高坏 (紀伊風土記の丘展示).JPG|土師器 高坏
{{small|[[和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘]]企画展示時に撮影。}} ファイル:塚穴山古墳出土 二次遺物.JPG|後世の遺物群

Overview of the stone chamber |alternate_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 36.03 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |burial goods = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |type = Circular mound |map_dot_label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and takes the type of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukaanayama Kofun}} {{citation needed|Tsukaana-yama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tennōji history]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan tombs]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] [[sn:sukaanayama Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|塚穴山古墳|version=99995250|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and takes the type of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴山古墳|version=99995250|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tennōji history]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan tombs]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] [[sn:sukaanayama Kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alternate_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōji|qq=}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 36.03 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |burial goods = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/7th century|qq=}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |type = Circular mound |map_dot_label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōji|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}} and takes the type of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}. It is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Basin|qq=}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibutai Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Moriya|qq=}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴山古墳|version=99995250|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tennōji history]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan tombs]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] [[sn:sukaanayama Kofun]]

Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibutai Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Moriya|qq=}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴山古墳|version=99995250|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tennōji history]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] [[sn:sukaanayama Kofun]]

Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |burial goods = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/7th century|qq=}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |type = Circular mound |map_dot_label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tennōji|ja|天理市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōji|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}} and takes the type of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}. It is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Basin|qq=}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibutai Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Moriya|qq=}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴山古墳|version=99995250|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] [[sn:sukaanayama Kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alternate_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tennōji]], Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 36.03 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |burial goods = Iron swords, iron sickles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |accompanying = |period = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |type = Circular mound |map_dot_label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tennōji]], Nara Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] and takes the type of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}. It is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]] and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]]. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴山古墳|version=99995250|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] [[sn:sukaanayama Kofun]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alternate_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tennōji]], Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 36.03 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |burial goods = Iron swords, iron sickles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |accompanying = |period = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |type = Circular mound |map_dot_label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tennōji]], Nara Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] and takes the type of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}. It is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]] and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]]. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴山古墳|version=99995250|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] [[sn:sukaanayama Kofun]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:杣之内古墳群]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace redirect category → Kofun of Nara prefecture
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alternate_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tennōji]], Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 36.03 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Ishibutai style); contains a stone coffin |burial goods = Iron swords, iron sickles, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |accompanying = |period = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |type = Circular mound |map_dot_label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tennōji]], Nara Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] and takes the type of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}. It is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]] and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]]. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a stone coffin painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴山古墳|version=99995250|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsukaanayama Kofun]] [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] [[sn:sukaanayama Kofun]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:杣之内古墳群]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → History of Tenri, Nara
revision.text:
