Overview of the stone chamber |alternate_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tennōji}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 36.03 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Ishibutai style); contains a Sarcophagus |burial goods = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |type = Circular mound |map_dot_label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tennōji}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the type of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|WD=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a Sarcophagus painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴山古墳|version=99995250|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] {{Commons category|Tsukaanayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q81256594}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[ja:塚穴山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[ja:塚穴山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsukaanayama Kofun]][[ja:塚穴山古墳]] {{Otheruses||Tsukaanayama Kofun in Kashiba, Nara Prefecture|[[Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun|平野塚穴山古墳]]}} {{infobox Kofun |name = Tsukaanayama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the stone chamber |alternate_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tennōji}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 36.03 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Ishibutai style); contains a Sarcophagus |burial goods = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |type = Circular mound |map_dot_label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tennōji}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the type of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|WD=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a Sarcophagus painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴山古墳|version=99995250|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] {{Commons category|Tsukaanayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q81256594}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Overview of the stone chamber |alternate_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tennōji|1=Tenri, Nara}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 36.03 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Ishibutai style); contains a Sarcophagus |burial goods = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |type = Circular mound |map_dot_label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tennōji|1=Tenri, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the type of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|WD=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun|1=Ishibutai Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a Sarcophagus painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴山古墳|version=99995250|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] {{Commons category|Tsukaanayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q81256594}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Overview of the stone chamber |alternate_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tennōji|1=Tenri, Nara}}, Madara Town, Tsukaanayama, Nara Prefecture |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 36.03 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 31.60 |iso = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: approx. 63.4 m (restored estimate); Height: over 8 m |burial = Two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Ishibutai style); contains a Sarcophagus |burial goods = Iron swords, iron sickles, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |accompanying = |period = {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |buried = Speculated to be Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari |heritage = |cultural = |remarks = |map = Japan Nara |type = Circular mound |map_dot_label = Tsukaanayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukaanayama Kofun''' (塚穴山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tennōji|1=Tenri, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and takes the type of a {{ill|circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Situated at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}, the large circular tumulus is located at the edge of a hill (dike) north of the {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound has been significantly destroyed, with the upper and northern halves exposed. Excavations were conducted in 1964 (Shōwa 39) and 1988 (Shōwa 63). The estimated original size of the circular mound is over 60 meters in diameter (restored estimate: 63.4 meters), making it one of the largest circular mounds of its time in Japan. Surrounding the mound is a 9.5-13 meter-wide moat and outer embankment, giving the entire structure, including the moat and embankment, an external diameter of 112 meters. The burial facility comprises a two-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, featuring massive stones and opening southward. It shares characteristics with the {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|WD=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun|1=Ishibutai Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}} and has a total length of 17.12 meters, Ranking among the largest chambers in Japan. burial goods discovered include fragments of iron swords and other grave goods, though most were looted. The mound is speculated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} in the 7th century. Some researchers identify it as the tomb of Mononobe no Ōichi Mikari, the elder brother of {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}. == Features == File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat (right: mound; left: outer embankment) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (viewed toward the rear wall) File:Tsukaanayama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (viewed toward the opening) == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the site include Sue pottery, haniwa fragments, and a Sarcophagus painted with vermillion on the inside. Some later burial goods, such as cremation remains and medieval pottery, were also discovered, suggesting secondary usage of the tomb as a burial ground in medieval times. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321710179.html Tsukaanayama Kofun - Tennōji City Website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴山古墳|version=99995250|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚穴山古墳]] {{Commons category|Tsukaanayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q81256594}} [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
